facade_description: 40 Lippincott St presents a bay-and-gable domestic facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include original windows. HCD note: The property at 24 Kensington Avenue has value as an early-twentieth-century vernacular style twostorey commercial addition to a nineteenth-century residential structure that retains legibility of its original form.
